summary: Decomp/matching PR for the mpIsland map-collision module. The single changed file, src/melee/mp/mpisland.c, replaces raw decompiler temporaries in mpIsland_8005A728 with typed map/collision traversal, implements the formerly stubbed mpIsland_8005B004, and makes small matching/data-order cleanups. The PR body only says "tracking PR", so intent is inferred from the diff and decomp.dev report.
what_changed: Added <baselib/debug.h> and used HSD_ASSERT in newly typed code paths.
Moved the address-commented static float mpIsland_804D8158 near the other mpIsland globals before mpIsland_80458E88.
Rewrote mpIsland_8005A728 from raw short/int pointer arithmetic and commented-out offset writes into named MapCollData, CollLine, CollVtx, and mp_UnkStruct0 operations.

mpIsland_8005A728 now explicitly processes floor and ceiling segments using floor_count/floor_start and ceiling_count/ceiling_start, a visited[0x600] bitmap, LINE_FLAG_ENABLED, LINE_FLAG_HIDDEN, CollLine_Floor, CollLine_Ceiling, next_id0/prev_id0 links, and mpJointFromLine.

Implemented mpIsland_8005B004 in C, replacing the /// #mpIsland_8005B004 placeholder. The implementation rebuilds/reuses mp_UnkStruct0 entries for a CollJoint's dynamic line range, filters lines by (flags & 0x1F) == (arg3 | 0x10), follows prev/next links, handles loop cases, and populates endpoints/Vec3 fields from CollVtx data.
Made a small local declaration-order change in mpIsland_8005ACE8 by declaring CollJoint* temp_r3 with the other locals before assignment.

decomp.dev reported mpisland .sdata and .sdata2 reaching 100%, mpIsland_8005ACE8 reaching 100%, mpIsland_8005A728 improving from 53.62% to 90.36%, and mpIsland_8005B004 improving from 0.00% to 94.81%.
smart_moves: Converted decompiler-style casts and offset math into existing collision-domain structs, making semantics searchable without renaming still-unknown mp_UnkStruct0 fields prematurely.
Preserved low-level matching details while improving readability: HSD_MemAlloc(0x2C), explicit field stores, visited bitmap size, HSD_ASSERT line numbers, and stack padding were kept visible.
Separated floor and ceiling traversal into parallel typed blocks, making the endpoint direction differences clear: floors use start v0/end v1, while ceilings use start v1/end v0.
Used project flag names for known bits, especially LINE_FLAG_ENABLED, LINE_FLAG_HIDDEN, CollLine_Floor, and CollLine_Ceiling, instead of leaving raw 0x10000/0x40000/hi_flags tests everywhere.

In mpIsland_8005B004, first moves existing nodes for the target joint into a reuse/free list before allocating new mp_UnkStruct0 nodes, preserving original list-recycling behavior instead of always allocating.
Kept PAD_STACK next to the 0x600-byte visited buffers, signaling that stack-frame shape was part of the match strategy.
decomp_lessons: When mpLib_8004D164 data is being indexed as shorts, try mapping it to MapCollData fields before continuing pointer arithmetic; floor_start/floor_count and ceiling_start/ceiling_count made mpIsland_8005A728 much clearer.
For ground collision line chains, MapLine next_id0 and prev_id0 plus hi_flags are enough to express floor/ceiling continuation checks in readable C.
CollLine flags and MapLine hi_flags represent different concepts in this code: flags are used for enabled/hidden/type filtering, while hi_flags are used to test CollLine_Floor or CollLine_Ceiling.
A fixed visited[0x600] byte array plus post-segment skipping is a recurring tactic for line-chain grouping; preserve its exact size and scan structure when matching related map code.
For matching partial decomp functions, it can be valuable to land a typed 90-95% implementation instead of leaving a /// # stub, especially when it documents control flow and struct relationships.
Do not over-normalize unknown list globals too early. mpIsland_80458E88 still has xN fields, but typed local names and member usage expose likely head/tail semantics for later cleanup.
naming_conventions: Existing symbol-address naming was retained: mpIsland_8005A728, mpIsland_8005B004, mpIsland_8005ACE8, mpIsland_804D8158, and mpIsland_80458E88.
Domain locals use concise collision/map terms: map, lines, vtx, joints, inner, line_idx, end_idx, start_idx, end_total, visited, hidden, type_flag, link, and z_val.
The allocated island segment node is named mpisp, matching the original assert concept/string while using mp_UnkStruct0* as the typed pointer.
Known collision constants are named through project macros/enums: LINE_FLAG_ENABLED, LINE_FLAG_HIDDEN, CollLine_Floor, and CollLine_Ceiling.
Unknown struct fields remain xN-style, such as mp_UnkStruct0.x20/x24/x26/x28 and mpIsland_80458E88.x8/xC, rather than inventing names without stronger evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x10) in mpIsland_8005A728 and PAD_STACK(16) in mpIsland_8005B004 after u8 visited[0x600] to preserve stack layout.
Kept explicit memzero(visited, 0x600u) and pointer-based visited scanning loops instead of simplifying to higher-level iteration.
Used HSD_ASSERT with observed line constants such as 62, 0x3E, and 0x206 after adding <baselib/debug.h>.
Preserved HSD_MemAlloc(0x2C) and direct member-by-member initialization of mp_UnkStruct0 nodes, which likely helps instruction order and register allocation.
mpIsland_8005B004 uses explicit infinite loops, link break checks, a loop flag, and a goto label after_alloc, suggesting control-flow shape was chosen for matching.
Small local declaration ordering in mpIsland_8005ACE8 was enough for the bot to report the function moving from 99.81% to 100%.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the PR slice.
The PR body only says "tracking PR", so there is no explicit author explanation beyond the title and diff.

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 59.08% (+0.09%, +3644 bytes) and matched data 37.69% (+48 bytes). The bot report also listed unrelated itsamusgrapple matches, but the only changed source file in this PR was mpisland.c.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for mp_UnkStruct0 field renames, especially x20, x24, x26, x28, and the mpIsland_80458E88 list fields.
Compare later mpIsland_8005B004 work to see what closed the remaining gap from 94.81% to a full match.
Verify whether mpIsland_8005B004's unused arg4 and arg5 receive names or semantic explanations in later decomp work.
Look for other map-collision functions that group lines with visited[0x600], next_id0/prev_id0, LINE_FLAG_ENABLED, and LINE_FLAG_HIDDEN.
Audit the mpIsland_8005A728 floor/ceiling head-tail assignments against later cleanup before renaming mpIsland_80458E88.x8 or xC.
